findings.evidence: `mathlib/Mathlib/MeasureTheory/Measure/CharacteristicFunction/Basic.lean:160-163` defines `charFun_eq_fourierIntegral' (t : E) : charFun μ t = VectorFourier.fourierIntegral fourierChar μ (innerₗ E) 1 (-(2 * π)⁻¹ • t)`, which yields the new theorem by taking `t = -(2 * π) • a`.
findings.fix: Reuse `charFun_eq_fourierIntegral'` at call sites, or replace this declaration with documentation/examples showing the one-line rewrite if the roadmap only needs the convention recorded.
findings.issue: `fourierIntegral_one_eq_charFun` is just `MeasureTheory.charFun_eq_fourierIntegral'` specialized to `t = -(2 * Real.pi) • a` and reversed after scalar simplification.

summary: The remaining API is a named specialization/reorientation of Mathlib's existing characteristic-function/Fourier-integral bridge, so this PR still adds parallel surface for an already available conversion.
